  with Physical\_HN as a separate archetype.}
  $\beta^{\uparrow}$, $\beta^{\downarrow}$: directional friction parameters
  re-estimated with Physical\_HN separated from Physical\_Terminal as a fourth archetype.
  Panel A: source occupation and skill fixed effects.
  Panel B: target occupation and skill fixed effects.
  SC\_Specialized is the reference archetype.
  95\% CI in brackets ($\pm 1.96$ SE$_c$); standard errors clustered three-way.
  Full risk set (not subsampled). $^{***}p<0.001$.}

Archetype & $\beta^{\uparrow}$ & $\beta^{\downarrow}$ & $\beta^{\uparrow}$ & $\beta^{\downarrow}$ \\
\midrule
\multicolumn{5}{l}{\textit{Adoption}} \\
SC_General & 0.191*** [0.123, 0.259] & 0.129** [0.037, 0.222] & 0.150*** [0.091, 0.209] & 0.002ns [-0.044, 0.047] \\
SC_Specialized & 0.245*** [0.159, 0.331] & 0.227*** [0.122, 0.333] & 0.185*** [0.110, 0.260] & 0.107** [0.041, 0.173] \\

\bottomrule
\multicolumn{5}{p{14cm}}{\footnotesize Note:
  Both Physical\_Terminal and Physical\_HN carry negative $\beta^{\uparrow}$
  in both panels and both flows, confirming that the upward barrier for
  sensory/physical requirements holds regardless of nestedness position
  within the domain. Physical\_HN shows a somewhat attenuated barrier
  relative to Physical\_Terminal, consistent with the boundary-case
  interpretation in Section~S8.}
